cards.title: Christopher Nolan Warns AI Is a Trojan Horse
cards.body: The 'Odyssey' director compares AI to the famous Greek deception, suggesting hidden risks within. "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside," he cautions.

cards.title: Nonprofit Current AI Builds Inclusive Global AI Web
cards.body: Current AI, a nonprofit, is developing AI infrastructure that serves all cultures equally across devices and chat platforms. The project aims to democratize AI access worldwide.

cards.title: Moonshot AI Releases New Kimi Model Version
cards.body: The Chinese company unveiled an updated Kimi model this week, sparking debate about global AI development. Concerns about competitive AI systems have intensified discussions.

cards.title: VC Neil Rimer Predicts AI Wealth Redistribution
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder argues historic AI-generated wealth must be redistributed, voluntarily or otherwise. The prediction signals growing pressure on tech industry inequality.

cards.title: Vertu Luxury AI Agent Costs $6,880
cards.body: Vertu's premium foldable device includes an AI agent, but real-world performance questions remain. Battery life, security, and workflow integration all face scrutiny.

cards.title: Databricks Reaches $188 Billion Valuation
cards.body: The data company has repositioned itself as an AI leader with published research on cost savings from open-weight models. Databricks extends its dominance in AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Zoom Meeting Transcription Raises Privacy Concerns
cards.body: As every conversation gets transcribed and summarized, questions emerge about who actually reads all this data. The practice highlights AI's surveillance implications.

cards.title: Agility Robotics Opens Fremont Training Center
cards.body: Agility is establishing a new training facility for its Digit robots in Tesla's backyard. The move signals expansion into core robotics manufacturing markets.

cards.title: AI Memory Demand Slows India Smartphone Market
cards.body: India's smartphone slowdown reveals how AI is reshaping consumer electronics demand and pricing. The boom fundamentally alters corporate strategy across the region.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple filed a major trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct involving its chief hardware officer. The complaint claims over 400 Apple employees now work at OpenAI.

cards.title: Patreon Blocks AI Bots From Scraping Content
cards.body: Patreon partnered with Cloudflare to actively block AI training bots instead of relying solely on robots.txt. The move strengthens creator protections against unauthorized model training.

cards.title: Apple's Lawsuit Disrupts OpenAI's IPO Plans
cards.body: The timing of Apple's trade secrets lawsuit couldn't be worse for OpenAI's public offering prospects. Legal uncertainty now clouds the company's near-term trajectory.

cards.title: GPU Financiers Pivot to $400M Inference Chips
cards.body: A $400 million chip-backed loan signals the next wave of AI infrastructure investment shifting toward inference. The deal highlights changing capital priorities in AI.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds Personalized AI Avatars
cards.body: Google is launching personalized AI avatars in Vids that let users star in their own videos. Gemini Omni-powered tools generate and edit videos from prompts.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game-Creation Feature
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature generates basic games from a single text prompt on mobile. The tool democratizes game creation for non-technical users.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Links and Interacts With Apps
cards.body: Google expanded AI Mode to complete tasks across frequently-used applications beyond simple Q&A. The update positions AI assistants as active workflow agents.

cards.title: DoorDash Opens Command-Line Ordering Tool
cards.body: DoorDash launched dd-cli, letting developers and AI agents order food from the terminal. The beta marks infrastructure designed specifically for AI agents.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases Hardware Including ChatGPT Basketball
cards.body: OpenAI launched its first hardware product this week, including an unexpected ChatGPT-branded basketball. The merchandising move signals brand expansion beyond software.

cards.title: Former DeepMind Researcher Raises at $300M Pre-Seed
cards.body: Andrew Dai, who helped build influential AI systems including ChatGPT research, raised $300M before launching a product. He believes visual AI represents the next major frontier.

cards.title: Christopher Nolan Warns AI is a 'Trojan Horse'
cards.body: Director Christopher Nolan compared AI to the Trojan horse, cautioning that the technology contains hidden risks. His comments reflect growing concerns about AI's potential consequences.

cards.title: Nonprofit Current AI Builds Inclusive Global AI Web
cards.body: Current AI, a nonprofit organization, is developing AI infrastructure designed to be free and accessible to all cultures and communities. The project has made progress across devices and AI chat platforms.

cards.title: Moonshot AI Releases New Kimi Model in China
cards.body: Chinese company Moonshot AI released an updated version of its Kimi model this week. The release has sparked discussions about competitive AI development outside Western markets.

cards.title: Databricks Reaches $188B Valuation as AI Leader
cards.body: Databricks has solidified its position as one of AI's most valued companies after pivoting its identity toward the AI boom. The company has published research on cost savings from open-weight AI models for coding.

cards.title: Apple Files Trade Secrets Lawsuit Against OpenAI
cards.body: Apple sued OpenAI alleging misconduct and claiming over 400 former Apple employees now work there. The lawsuit comes at a critical time as OpenAI pursues IPO plans.

cards.title: Patreon Actively Blocks AI Scraping Bots
cards.body: Patreon partnered with Cloudflare to block bots that scrape creator content for AI model training without permission. This marks a shift from passive robots.txt defenses to active blocking technology.

cards.title: AI Infrastructure Moves to Inference Chip Financing
cards.body: A $400 million chip-backed loan signals the next wave of AI infrastructure investment focused on inference chips. The deal reflects shifting priorities in AI hardware financing away from GPUs alone.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds Personalized AI Avatar Features
cards.body: Google expanded Vids with AI avatars that let users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. Gemini Omni-powered tools also enable video generation and editing from text prompts.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game Creation on Mobile
cards.body: Roblox introduced a "Build" feature enabling users to generate basic games using a single text prompt. The tool democratizes game creation within the platform.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Expands to Task Automation
cards.body: Google extended its AI Mode to complete tasks across apps beyond just answering questions. The update marks AI's move into practical workflow automation.

cards.title: DoorDash CLI Enables AI Agent Ordering
cards.body: DoorDash opened a beta for dd-cli, a command-line tool letting developers and AI agents search stores and place orders. The tool represents software designed specifically for AI agent interactions.

cards.title: Former DeepMind Researcher Launches AI Startup
cards.body: Andrew Dai, who contributed to AI systems including research informing ChatGPT, raised funding at a $300M pre-seed valuation. He believes visual AI represents the next frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red AI Security Tool
cards.body: OpenAI developed GPT-Red, an LLM designed as a cyber adversary to test and improve model defenses against attacks. The tool helped strengthen GPT-5.6's security capabilities.

cards.title: Anthropic Discovers New Window Into Claude's Thinking
cards.body: Anthropic announced a breakthrough discovering new insights into how its AI model Claude processes information. The research adds to understanding of AI model interpretability.

cards.title: AI May Fix or Worsen Prior Authorization Problem
cards.body: AI deployment in healthcare's prior authorization system could simplify or complicate the approval process. The outcome depends on how healthcare systems implement the technology.

cards.title: San Francisco Orders Removal of Nudify Apps
cards.body: San Francisco directed Apple and Google to remove AI-powered nudification apps from their app stores. The action targets tools that use AI to create non-consensual intimate images.

cards.title: EU Forces Google to Share Search Data and AI
cards.body: The EU mandated that Google share search data and open up its AI technology on Android. The ruling represents significant regulatory pressure on major AI companies.
